howItWorks.intro: This anxiety score calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Based on GAD-7 screening principles (simplified 5-item version)
Rate each item: 0=not at all, 1=several days, 2=more than half, 3=nearly every day
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is the GAD-7?
faq.answer: The GAD-7 (Generalized Anxiety Disorder 7) is a validated screening tool used by healthcare providers worldwide. It asks about anxiety symptoms over the past 2 weeks. A score of 10+ suggests clinical anxiety warranting professional evaluation.

faq.question: Is anxiety normal?
faq.answer: Some anxiety is normal and even helpful (motivating preparation, alerting to danger). Anxiety becomes a concern when it's excessive, hard to control, interferes with daily life, or causes physical symptoms (rapid heart rate, trouble breathing, insomnia).

faq.question: When should I seek help for anxiety?
faq.answer: Seek professional help if: anxiety interferes with work, relationships, or daily activities; you avoid situations due to fear; you have panic attacks; physical symptoms persist (insomnia, GI issues, muscle tension); or you're using substances to cope.

faq.question: Is the Anxiety Score Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard mental health and wellness formulas trusted by individuals seeking balance. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Anxiety Score Calculator?
sections.body: The Anxiety Score Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals seeking balance who need quick, accurate calculations in the mental health and wellness space. By entering your feeling nervous/anxious, can't stop worrying, trouble relaxing, you get instant results including anxiety score, severity, recommendation. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting anxiety score right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In mental health and wellness, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals seeking balance worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to support your mental well-being with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your feeling nervous/anxious and need to find the right anxiety score. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Feeling nervous/anxious (0-3)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your feeling nervous/anxious — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: can't stop worrying, trouble relaxing, easily irritated, feeling afraid. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your anxiety score, severity, recommendation. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your feeling nervous/anxious before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both anxiety score and severity — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to support your mental well-being.
If you're unsure about your feeling afraid, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
